input_payload: {"direction": "Compare gradient-boosted trees against deep learning for tabular EHR risk prediction in heart-failure cohorts.", "lit_digest": {"summary": "Shwartz-Ziv & Armon (2022) showed XGBoost matches or beats deep tabular models on most datasets. Gorishniy et al. (2021) introduced FT-Transformer for tabular data.", "key_works": [{"title": "Tabular Data: Deep Learning is Not All You Need", "authors": ["Shwartz-Ziv", "Armon"], "year": 2022, "doi_or_arxiv": "10.1016/j.inffus.2021.11.011", "relevance": "high"}], "gaps": ["EHR-specific evaluations are rare; most benchmarks are non-clinical"], "consensus": ["GBT remains competitive on tabular"], "disagreements": ["Mixed results when categorical cardinality is high"], "tools_needed": false}}
expected_summary: Five ideas grounded in the EHR / GBT literature. Ideas should clearly identify whether a public EHR dataset (MIMIC-IV, eICU) or a private one is needed; if private, the agent should mark grounding=weak per its hard rule.
notes: Off the medical-imaging domain — checks the agent doesn't fall back to chest-x-ray boilerplate. Score lower if the agent ignores the domain shift.
